steps: Go to https://dashboard.onesignal.com and log in or create a free account
Create a new app or select an existing one from the dashboard
In the app, go to Settings in the left sidebar
Your REST API Key is displayed under 'REST API Key'
Click the copy icon to copy the key
Use the key with the 'Basic' prefix in the Authorization header: 'Authorization: Basic <your-key>'

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: REST API Key
credential_fields.description: Your OneSignal app REST API Key, used with the 'Basic' prefix in the Authorization header

notes: Free tier available with unlimited web push notifications
Each app has its own REST API Key — make sure to use the key for the correct app
You also need the App ID (not secret) for most API requests — found on the same Keys & IDs page
You can create up to 16 API keys per app and configure IP allowlisting

test_endpoint.description: List all OneSignal apps for the authenticated account
